items.title: Women Reservation in India: A Legal Perspective
items.summary:
The article explains how the Constitution (One Hundred and Sixth Amendment) Act, 2023 introduced one-third reservation for women in Parliament and State Assemblies. It highlights the constitutional basis of affirmative action and its role in improving women’s political participation.
The post Women Reservation in India: A Legal Perspective appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Section 54F Exemption Allowed as Entire Capital Gain Was Invested Within Statutory Period
items.summary:
ITAT Chandigarh held that exemption under Section 54F cannot be denied merely because the assessee failed to deposit unutilised funds in the Capital Gain Account Scheme before the due date under Section 139(1). The Tribunal ruled that actual investment in a residential house within the prescribed period amounted to substantive compliance deserving liberal interpretation.
The post Section 54F Exemption Allowed as Entire Capital Gain Was Invested…

items.title: Section 69A Addition Deleted Because Cross-Examination of Third-Party Witness Was Denied
items.summary:
ITAT Mumbai held that an addition under Section 69A cannot be sustained when the assessee is denied the opportunity to cross-examine the person whose statement forms the basis of the assessment. The Tribunal ruled that reliance on such untested statements violates principles of natural justice and renders the addition legally unsustainable.
The post Section 69A Addition Deleted Because Cross-Examination of Third-Party Witness Was Denied…

items.title: ROC Imposes Penalty for Missing PAN and Email Details in PAS-3 Filing
items.summary:
ROC Chennai penalised a company and its director for failing to disclose PAN and e-mail IDs of allottees in Form PAS-3. The order highlights strict compliance requirements under Rule 14(6) of the Companies Act framework.
The post ROC Imposes Penalty for Missing PAN and Email Details in PAS-3 Filing appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ROC Imposes Penalty for Failure to Furnish PAN & Email IDs in PAS-3
items.summary:
ROC Chennai penalised a Nidhi company and its directors for incomplete allottee disclosures in Form PAS-3. The order clarifies that absence of PAN or e-mail details cannot excuse statutory non-compliance.
The post ROC Imposes Penalty for Failure to Furnish PAN & Email IDs in PAS-3 appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ROC Imposes Penalty as PAS-3 Did Not Mention Occupation of Allottees
items.summary:
ROC Chennai penalised a Nidhi company and its directors for filing incomplete allottee details in Form PAS-3. The ruling clarifies that occupation details of allottees are mandatory under Rule 12(2) of the Companies Rules, 2014.
The post ROC Imposes Penalty as PAS-3 Did Not Mention Occupation of Allottees appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ROC Imposes Penalty Due to 513-Day Delay in Filing Annual Return MGT-7
items.summary:
ROC Chennai penalised a company and its director for filing Form MGT-7 more than 500 days late under Section 92 of the Companies Act, 2013. The ruling confirms that annual return filing delays attract substantial penalties despite operational or legal difficulties.
The post ROC Imposes Penalty Due to 513-Day Delay in Filing Annual Return MGT-7 appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ROC Imposes Penalty Due to 313-Day Delay in Filing MGT-14 for Board Resolution
items.summary:
ROC Chennai penalised a company and its director for delayed filing of Form MGT-14 relating to approval of financial statements and Board’s Report. The ruling reiterates that board resolutions under Section 179(3) must be filed within the prescribed statutory timeline.
The post ROC Imposes Penalty Due to 313-Day Delay in Filing MGT-14 for Board Resolution appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ROC Imposes Penalty Due to 274-Day Delay in Filing AOC-4 Financial Statements
items.summary:
ROC Chennai penalised a company and its director for delayed filing of financial statements under Section 137 of the Companies Act, 2013. The ruling confirms that prolonged litigation and staff shortage cannot excuse statutory MCA filing delays.
The post ROC Imposes Penalty Due to 274-Day Delay in Filing AOC-4 Financial Statements appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ROC Imposes Penalty for Incorrect CSR Disclosure in AOC-4 XBRL Filing
items.summary:
ROC Mumbai levied a penalty under Section 450 after a company incorrectly stated that CSR provisions were not applicable in Form AOC-4 XBRL. The order highlights that directors signing MCA forms are personally responsible for accuracy of disclosures and attachments.
The post ROC Imposes Penalty for Incorrect CSR Disclosure in AOC-4 XBRL Filing appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Wrong Approval, Wrong Reassessment – Pune ITAT Quashes 148 Notice for Taking Sanction From PCIT Instead of PCCIT!
items.summary:
The Pune ITAT held that reassessment proceedings become void when approval under Section 151 is taken from the wrong authority. Since sanction was obtained from the PCIT instead of the PCCIT, the notice under Section 148 was quashed.
The post Wrong Approval, Wrong Reassessment – Pune ITAT Quashes 148 Notice for Taking Sanction From PCIT Instead of PCCIT! appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: No Addition on “Excel Sheet Theory” Alone – ITAT Mumbai Deletes ₹64 Lakh Alleged On-Money Addition in Rubberwala Group Case!
items.summary:
The Mumbai ITAT held that additions under Section 69 cannot be sustained merely on the basis of uncorroborated excel-sheet entries and third-party statements. The Tribunal deleted the alleged on-money addition in the Rubberwala Group matter.
The post No Addition on “Excel Sheet Theory” Alone – ITAT Mumbai Deletes ₹64 Lakh Alleged On-Money Addition in Rubberwala Group Case! appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Why Section 76 of GST Law Protects Bona Fide Purchasers from ITC Denial
items.summary:
The article argues that Section 76 of the CGST Act provides a complete recovery mechanism against defaulting suppliers, making ITC denial to genuine purchasers legally unsustainable. It analyses constitutional principles, High Court rulings and statutory safeguards under GST law.
The post Why Section 76 of GST Law Protects Bona Fide Purchasers from ITC Denial appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: E-Invoicing under GST: Applicability, Legal Framework & Exemptions – A review
items.summary:
This article explains the legal framework governing e-invoicing under GST, including turnover thresholds, IRN requirements and sector-wise exemptions. It also discusses the consequences of non-compliance under Rule 48 of the CGST Rules.
The post E-Invoicing under GST: Applicability, Legal Framework & Exemptions – A review appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: OFAC Sanctions, Iran Imports & ₹6.75 Crore Consultancy Fee – ITAT Mumbai Says Revenue Cannot Sit in the Businessman’s Chair!
items.summary:
The Mumbai ITAT allowed deduction of professional fees paid for facilitating remittances relating to Iranian-origin imports affected by OFAC sanctions. The Tribunal held that the expenditure was incurred wholly and exclusively for business purposes.
The post OFAC Sanctions, Iran Imports & ₹6.75 Crore Consultancy Fee – ITAT Mumbai Says Revenue Cannot Sit in the Businessman’s Chair! appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Reassessment Quashed as Section 148 Approval Was Taken from Wrong Authority
items.summary:
The Bombay High Court held that reopening for AY 2016–17 becomes invalid when sanction is obtained from an authority not prescribed under Section 151(2). The Supreme Court affirmed the ruling by dismissing the Revenue’s SLP on delay and merits.
The post Reassessment Quashed as Section 148 Approval Was Taken from Wrong Authority appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: The Gold Duty Shock: A Symptom, Not the Disease
items.summary:
The article argues that the sharp increase in gold import duty was triggered by pressure on India’s forex reserves, rising oil prices, and widening dollar outflows. It claims the move may revive smuggling, hurt the jewellery sector, and expose deeper weaknesses in economic management.
The post The Gold Duty Shock: A Symptom, Not the Disease appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Demonetisation Sales Can’t Become “Bogus” Just Because SBNs Were Accepted- ITAT Bangalore Deletes ₹29.27 Lakh Addition!
items.summary:
The Bangalore ITAT held that genuine business sales recorded in audited books cannot be treated as unexplained cash credits merely because payment was received in Specified Bank Notes during demonetisation. The Tribunal deleted the ₹29.27 lakh addition under Section 68.
The post Demonetisation Sales Can’t Become “Bogus” Just Because SBNs Were Accepted- ITAT Bangalore Deletes ₹29.27 Lakh Addition! appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: SEBI Moves to Align Listed SDI Framework with RBI’s Standard Asset Directions
items.summary:
SEBI proposes SDI rule changes to align listed securitisation norms with RBI directions, covering SPDE governance, disclosures, trustee changes, and more.
The post SEBI Moves to Align Listed SDI Framework with RBI’s Standard Asset Directions appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Rationalisation of AOC-4 Filing Framework under Companies Act, 2013
items.summary:
A Discussion Perspective A company is owned by its shareholders and managed by its Board of Directors. However, the framework of corporate governance extends beyond the objective of profit generation alone. Companies today operate within a broader stakeholder ecosystem comprising employees, lenders, creditors, investors, customers, regulators, government authorities and society at large. Contemporary corporate governance […]
The post…

items.title: What Every CA Student Should Learn After Exams (Before Placements Begin)
items.summary:
Learn Excel, LinkedIn, interview skills, GST, Ind AS, and tech tools after CA exams to improve ICAI placement chances and secure better offers.
The post What Every CA Student Should Learn After Exams (Before Placements Begin) appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: New PAN Regulations for NRIs, Foreign Entities & Their Representatives
items.summary:
India’s new PAN compliance framework introduces stricter documentation and verification requirements for NRIs and foreign entities. The reforms replace old forms and mandate TIN, passport, and authorized representative details.
The post New PAN Regulations for NRIs, Foreign Entities & Their Representatives appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Technical Issues in GSTN’s New Annexure-B Utility
items.summary:
Taxpayers and professionals are facing major technical issues while uploading Annexure-B JSON files for GST refunds. Common problems include duplicate data errors, missing invoice validations, and failed JSON uploads despite correct data preparation.
The post Technical Issues in GSTN’s New Annexure-B Utility appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: GSTN Introduces Automated Annexure-B Utility for GST Refunds
items.summary:
GSTN has replaced manual Annexure-B filing with a JSON-based automated validation utility linked directly to Form RFD-01. The new system introduces invoice-level checks, ITC classification, and automated validations for refund processing.
The post GSTN Introduces Automated Annexure-B Utility for GST Refunds appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: GST Nil Returns: Everything You Need to Know (Without Headache)
items.summary:
The article explains when businesses must file a GST Nil Return despite having no sales, purchases, or tax liability. It highlights the importance of maintaining GST compliance to avoid penalties and future filing issues.
The post GST Nil Returns: Everything You Need to Know (Without Headache) appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Bamboo Tree & Professional Growth: A Lesson in Patience, Persistence & Silent Transformation
items.summary:
The article explains how the bamboo tree symbolizes invisible preparation before visible success in professional life. It highlights the importance of patience, discipline, and long-term learning for sustainable career growth.
The post Bamboo Tree & Professional Growth: A Lesson in Patience, Persistence & Silent Transformation appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: GSTAT vs CESTAT: Deciphering Jurisdiction for IGST on Import of Goods
items.summary:
The Andhra Pradesh High Court held that Customs authorities are responsible for levy and assessment of IGST on imported goods. The ruling has renewed debate over whether appeals should lie before GSTAT or CESTAT.
The post GSTAT vs CESTAT: Deciphering Jurisdiction for IGST on Import of Goods appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: GST Show Cause Notices can cover Multiple Financial Years: Karnataka HC
items.summary:
Karnataka High Court held that consolidated show cause notices under Sections 73 and 74 of the CGST Act can legally cover multiple financial years. The Court ruled that the law does not restrict GST notices to a single tax period.
The post GST Show Cause Notices can cover Multiple Financial Years: Karnataka HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Delhi HC: Additional Documents Can Be Filed in Criminal Tax Prosecution – “Truth Cannot Be Sacrificed for Technicalities”
items.summary:
The Delhi High Court held that additional documents already referred to in a criminal complaint can be filed later under Section 311 Cr.P.C. The Court ruled that procedural defects should not obstruct substantive justice where no serious prejudice is caused.
The post Delhi HC: Additional Documents Can Be Filed in Criminal Tax Prosecution – “Truth Cannot Be Sacrificed for Technicalities” appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Delhi HC to Tax Department: 100% Shareholding Doesn’t Make You Owner of Company’s Property
items.summary:
The Delhi High Court held that shareholders of a foreign company cannot be taxed on the company’s rental income and capital gains merely because they hold all its shares. The Court reiterated that a company is a separate legal entity unless fraud or sham arrangements are proved.
The post Delhi HC to Tax Department: 100% Shareholding Doesn’t Make You Owner of Company’s Property appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: SEBI Plans Cap on Commodity Derivatives Penalties Due to Concerns Over Excessive Punishment
items.summary:
The regulator has proposed capping penalties for position limit breaches in commodity derivatives, stating that excessive penalties may discourage market participation and risk management.
The post SEBI Plans Cap on Commodity Derivatives Penalties Due to Concerns Over Excessive Punishment appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: SEBI Proposes Phased Physical Settlement in Agricultural Commodity Derivatives
items.summary:
SEBI has proposed objective triggers such as average daily traded volume and open interest for transitioning agricultural derivatives to compulsory physical settlement. The proposal seeks to ensure that flexibility in settlement methodology remains limited and transparent.
The post SEBI Proposes Phased Physical Settlement in Agricultural Commodity Derivatives appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: PFRDA Introduces Additional Incentive Support for Grass-Root Pension Enrollment Network
items.summary:
PFRDA has expanded the NPS Sanchay incentive framework to include CSC-VLEs, BCs/Pension Sakhis, and PACS operating through PoPs. The move aims to strengthen last-mile pension outreach and improve pension inclusion.
The post PFRDA Introduces Additional Incentive Support for Grass-Root Pension Enrollment Network appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Important May 2026 Income Tax Compliances for Gems & Jewellery Businesses
items.summary:
The article explains how gems and jewellery businesses must distinguish compliances governed by the Income-tax Act, 1961 and the new Income-tax Act, 2025 during May 2026. It highlights important TDS, TCS, and reporting deadlines applicable during the transition period.
The post Important May 2026 Income Tax Compliances for Gems & Jewellery Businesses appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: From Compliance To Culture: Redefining POSH Governance In Modern Corporate Era
items.summary: From Compliance To Culture: Redefining POSH Governance In Modern Corporate Era A Governance-Centric Analysis of Workplace Safety, Accountability and Institutional Responsibility Introduction The modern corporate environment is witnessing a significant transformation in the understanding of workplace governance and employee protection. Issues relating to dignity, inclusion, ethics, and accountability are no longer viewed merely as human…

items.title: Insurer Cannot Escape Liability Without Evidence Against Driving Licence: AP HC
items.summary:
The Andhra Pradesh High Court held that an Insurance Company cannot avoid liability without producing evidence showing absence of a valid driving licence. The Court also upheld compensation based on gross salary and enhanced amounts under conventional heads.
The post Insurer Cannot Escape Liability Without Evidence Against Driving Licence: AP HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Bombay HC Quashes Reassessment as Limitation Expired Before Assessment Order Was Passed
items.summary:
The Bombay High Court held that reassessment proceedings became time-barred because no reassessment order was passed within the limitation period prescribed under Section 153. The Court ruled that procedural remand directions did not extend limitation under Section 153(6).
The post Bombay HC Quashes Reassessment as Limitation Expired Before Assessment Order Was Passed appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Customs Broker Liable for Penalty as Goods Were Transported to Unauthorised Premises
items.summary:
CESTAT Bangalore upheld penalty under Section 112(b) after finding that the Customs Broker knowingly arranged transportation of duty-free imported goods to locations not permitted under advance licences. The Tribunal held that such conduct amounted to aiding violation of customs conditions.
The post Customs Broker Liable for Penalty as Goods Were Transported to Unauthorised Premises appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Deletes Addition as Sale Value & Stamp Duty Alone Cannot Prove Cash Payment
items.summary:
ITAT Mumbai held that denial of cross-examination in a case based on third-party statements and seized records violated principles of natural justice. The matter was remanded for fresh assessment after granting the assessee an opportunity to cross-examine witnesses.
The post ITAT Deletes Addition as Sale Value & Stamp Duty Alone Cannot Prove Cash Payment appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Delhi Deletes IDS Addition as Income Was Taxed in Wrong Assessment Year
items.summary:
The ITAT Delhi held that undisclosed income declared under IDS-2016 but unpaid within prescribed time must be taxed in the year of declaration, not the original assessment year. The reassessment addition for AY 2013-14 was deleted.
The post ITAT Delhi Deletes IDS Addition as Income Was Taxed in Wrong Assessment Year appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: NCLT Cannot Keep Reserved IBC Matter Pending Indefinitely: Bombay HC
items.summary:
The Bombay High Court directed constitution of a special NCLT Bench after an insolvency petition remained pending despite being reserved for orders twice. The Court held that delay frustrated the purpose of IBC proceedings.
The post NCLT Cannot Keep Reserved IBC Matter Pending Indefinitely: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Residential Quarters for Employees Not Taxable as Commercial Construction: CESTAT Allahabad
items.summary:
Tribunal ruled that construction services relating to employee residential colonies were outside scope of commercial or industrial construction services during relevant period.
The post Residential Quarters for Employees Not Taxable as Commercial Construction: CESTAT Allahabad appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Kolkata Allows Foreign Tax Credit Despite Late Filing of Form 67
items.summary:
The ITAT Kolkata held that delayed filing of Form No. 67 is only a procedural defect and cannot deprive an assessee of Foreign Tax Credit under Section 90 and the India-USA DTAA.
The post ITAT Kolkata Allows Foreign Tax Credit Despite Late Filing of Form 67 appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: Quicklime With Less Than 98% Purity Falls Under CTH 2522: SC
items.summary:
The Supreme Court dismissed the Revenue’s appeals after observing that the classification dispute had already been settled in an earlier judgment upheld by the Court. The ruling confirms reliance on the precedent followed by CESTAT.
The post Quicklime With Less Than 98% Purity Falls Under CTH 2522: SC appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Delhi Rejects Revenue Appeal Over Presumptive Taxation & Jewellery Additions
items.summary:
The ITAT Delhi held that once income higher than the presumptive rate under Section 44AD was declared, the assessee was not required to maintain detailed books or expense records. The addition based on estimated expenditure was deleted.
The post ITAT Delhi Rejects Revenue Appeal Over Presumptive Taxation & Jewellery Additions appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Remands Section 54F Dispute Due to Non-Consideration of Evidence
items.summary:
The ITAT Delhi held that the lower authorities failed to properly consider the assessee’s submissions and documentary evidence in a Section 54F dispute. The matter was remanded to the Assessing Officer for fresh adjudication after granting proper hearing opportunity.
The post ITAT Remands Section 54F Dispute Due to Non-Consideration of Evidence appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: ITAT Rejects MAT Addition as Company Validly Opted for Section 115BAA Concessional taxation
items.summary:
ITAT Delhi held that MAT provisions under Section 115JB cannot apply once a company validly opts for concessional taxation under Section 115BAA. The Tribunal also relied on consistency as the department had accepted the option in earlier years.
The post ITAT Rejects MAT Addition as Company Validly Opted for Section 115BAA Concessional taxation appeared first on TaxGuru.

items.title: CBDT Notifies Form 10-IEA for Section 115BAC(6) Option; Filing Mandatory Before ITR Due Date
items.summary: CBDT has notified the new Form 10-IEA for taxpayers carrying on business who wish to opt out of the new default regime for AY 2026-27. The form must be filed on or before the due date u/s 139(1).

items.title: Supreme Court: Satisfaction Note Mandatory Before Section 153C Notice; Absence Renders Proceedings Void
items.summary: Three-judge bench holds that the satisfaction note is a jurisdictional pre-condition under Section 153C and must be recorded before issuance of notice, not after.

items.title: 55th GST Council: Prosecution Threshold Raised to Rs 3 Crore; Term Life Insurance Exempt from GST
items.summary: The 55th GST Council recommended raising the monetary threshold for prosecution under CGST Act from Rs 2 crore to Rs 3 crore. Term life insurance premiums proposed to be fully exempt.

items.title: ITAT Mumbai: Section 56(2)(x) Not Applicable in NCLT-Sanctioned Group Restructuring
items.summary: Special Bench holds that FMV deeming mechanism under Rule 11UA cannot override substance of a court-approved restructuring scheme. Addition of Rs 18.4 crore deleted.

items.title: CBIC Circular 231/2026: ITC Available on Canteen Services Under Factories Act Obligation
items.summary: CBIC ends long-standing uncertainty confirming GST Input Tax Credit is available on canteen services provided to factory workers under statutory obligation under Factories Act, 1948.

items.title: ITAT Mumbai: Reassessment Under Section 148 Quashed for Non-Compliance With Section 151A Faceless Mechanism
items.summary: Tribunal held that reassessment notice u/s 148 not issued through the mandatory faceless assessment mechanism under Section 151A is jurisdictionally invalid. Once the foundational jurisdiction is vitiated, the entire assessment including addition of Rs 1.61 crore u/s 68 cannot survive.

items.title: ITAT Chennai: Reassessment Notice u/s 148 Quashed – Approval from PCIT Instead of Specified Authority u/s 151(ii) Renders Notice Invalid
items.summary: Tribunal held that for reassessment notices issued beyond three years, approval must be obtained from the Specified Authority under Section 151(ii) and not from the Principal Commissioner of Income Tax. Approval by PCIT is without jurisdiction. Reassessment for AY 2016-17 involving Section 69A addition quashed.
items.source: ITAT Chennai – Aman Gupta vs DCIT

items.title: Gauhati HC: Show Cause Notice u/s 73 CGST Issued One Day Before Deadline Valid; Corresponding Date Rule Applied
items.summary: Gauhati High Court dismissed writ petition challenging SCN for excess ITC for FY 2020-21. Court applied the corresponding date rule from Dodds v. Walker and held that notice issued on 29.11.2024 for an order deadline of 28.02.2025 fully complies with the three-month prior notice requirement under Section 73(2) CGST Act.
items.source: Gauhati HC – Surya Business Pvt Ltd vs State of Assam

items.title: ITAT Delhi: Kanpur Development Authority Entitled to Exemption u/s 11; Infrastructure Funds Received Under Govt Orders Not Taxable
items.summary: Tribunal dismissed Revenue appeal holding that the Authority established for public development purposes qualifies for charitable exemption u/s 11. Infrastructure funds received under government orders and utilised per state committee directives were held to be non-taxable. Contribution to AwasBandhu allowed as permissible application of income.
items.source: ITAT Delhi – DCIT vs Kanpur Development Authority

items.title: Delhi HC: Reassessment Based on Client Code Modification Transactions Unsustainable Where Tax Already Paid at Highest Rate
items.summary: Delhi High Court dismissed Revenue appeal holding that reassessment proceedings lacked tangible material — no specific company or correct broker was identified by AO. Since assessee had already paid tax at the highest applicable rate, no income could be said to have escaped assessment.
items.source: Delhi HC – PCIT-7 vs Atul Goel
